data.title: Test bloat cleanup
data.description:
## Context

The DSO plugin has 583 test files, with ~40+ in tests/skills/ and tests/agents/ that are pure change-detection tests — grepping markdown files for keywords, checking section headings, asserting string presence. These tests are explicitly flagged as BAD PATTERNS in the RED test writer agent prompt but remain in the codebase. Specific examples: test_fix_bug_skill.py (1,368 lines, 20+ tests grepping SKILL.md), test_brainstorm_gap_analysis.py (197 lines, fuzzy phrase matching), test-reviewer-dimension-names.sh (50 lines, dimension name grep), test-reviewer-light-checklist.sh (193 lines, 15+ section heading checks). Cleanup is most effective after the behavioral testing standard (Epic 8df3-61d0) and enforcement (Epic 1f75-9b32) are in place, so new bloat does not accumulate during cleanup. This epic supersedes dso-4s8r (Cleanup tests).

## Success Criteria

- Existing bloated tests are triaged and either deleted (pure change-detection with no behavioral value) or replaced with behavioral alternatives (execute the script/tool and assert on observable output)
- Test count for the DSO plugin stabilizes or decreases after cleanup, with a measurable reduction in test files that grep markdown source content

## Dependencies

- Epic 8df3-61d0 (Behavioral Testing Standard) — so replacement tests follow the new standard
- Epic 1f75-9b32 (Test Quality Enforcement) — so the gate patterns inform what to delete and new bloat does not accumulate

## Approach

Triage the existing test suite using the pre-commit gate patterns from Epic 1f75-9b32 as the detection rubric. Priority order: (1) delete tests that grep/cat/read source .md files and assert on string content, (2) delete tests that check file/class existence as sole assertion, (3) replace tests where behavioral alternatives exist. Coordinate .test-index marker updates in the same commits as test deletions. Validate test suite health after each cleanup batch.

## Research Reference

See plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_RESEARCH.md and plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_SESSION.md for full research findings and session context.
